final_answer: COVID-19 primarily spreads through the air via droplets and aerosols when infected individuals cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk. The virus can land on the face of nearby individuals or be inhaled, especially in close proximity or poorly ventilated areas [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000, chunk_00008]. Close contact, such as sitting next to someone or sharing a car, increases transmission risk [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00011]. People can spread the virus even if they are asymptomatic [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]. Additionally, touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after contact with contaminated surfaces can also lead to infection [node_a:3:e765d8d1, node_c:2:5668c8d6].
